div.head: A
div.p: 1. Supreme Council of the Allies
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Office of the Minister)
President: M. Georges Clemenceau (France)
Secretary General: M. P. Dutasta (France)

p.hi: secretariat of the allied supreme council
collective secretariat (in charge of the minutes)

div.p: 2. Committee on the Verification of Powers

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. Arthur James Balfour;
The Rt. Hon. A. Bonar Law;
The Rt. Hon. G. N. Barnes;
The Rt. Hon. Lord Hardinge of Penshurst:

div.p: 3. Drafting Committee
President: M. Henri Fromageot (France)
B. COMMISSIONS AND COMMITTEES
I. Renewal of the Armistice With Germany
1. Control of the Production of Materials of War in Germany and the Disarmament of the German Army
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of January 24, 1919)
2. Specification of the Materials of War Which May Be Demanded From Germany
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of February 7, 1919)
3. Study of the Means of Imposing the Armistice Conditions on Germany
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of February 10, 1919)
President: Marshal Foch

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. the Lord Robert Cecil;

p.hi: members associated with the commission as a consequence of the decision of the supreme council of the allies

row.cell: Rear Admiral Sir George P. Hope.

div.p: 4. Determination of the Conditions of the Disarmament of Germany
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of February 12, 1919)
President: Marshal Foch
II. Questions Common to All the Treaties of Peace
(Plenary Session of the Conference of January 25, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Hotel de Crillon
President: The President of the United States of America

row.cell: The President of the United States;
The Rt. Hon. the Lord Robert Cecil;
Lieut. General the Rt. Hon. J. C. Smuts.

div.p: Liaison with the Secretariat General of the Conference: M. de Sillac.

p.hi: II. Responsibility of the Authors of the War and the Enforcement of Penalties

div.p: (Plenary Session of the Conference of January 25, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: M. V. Scialoja (Italy)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. Sir Gordon Hewart (British Empire) or
The Rt. Hon. Sir Gordon Hewart;
The Rt. Hon. W. F. Massey.

div.p: Secretary General: M. A. de Lapradelle (France)
Liaison with the Secretariat General of the Conference: Commandant M. Escoffier.
First Subcommission.—Criminal Acts
President: The Rt. Hon. W. F. Massey (British Empire)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. W. F. Massey.

div.p: Second Subcommission.—Responsibility of the Authors of the War
President: Sir Ernest Pollock (British Empire)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. Sir Gordon Hewart;

div.p: Third Subcommission.—Violation of the Laws and Customs of War
President: Hon. Robert Lansing (United States of America)
(Plenary Session of the Conference of January 25, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Finance, Hôtel du Ministre, Rue de Rivoli, Porte D.
President: M. L. L. Klotz (France)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. W. M. Hughes (British Empire);
Lieut. Colonel the Hon. Sidney Peel;
Lieut. Colonel the Hon. Sidney Peel;

div.p: Liaison with the Secretariat General of the Conference: Baron J. Pieyre
First Subcommission.—Evaluation of Damage
President: The Rt. Hon. the Lord Sumner of Ibstone (British Empire)
Vice President: M. E. Chiesa (Italy)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. the Lord Sumner of Ibstone.

div.p: Secretary: M. E. Minost (France)
Second Subcommission.—Study of Financial Capacity of Enemy States, Their Means of Payment and Reparation
President: The Rt. Hon. the Lord Cunliffe (British Empire)
Vice President: M. Loucheur (France)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. the Lord Cunliffe.

div.p: Secretary: M. F. Foberti (Italy)
Third Subcommission.—Measures of Control and Guarantees
President: The Rt. Hon. W. M. Hughes (British Empire)
Vice President: Mr. Bernard M. Baruch (United States of America)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. W. M. Hughes.

div.p: Secretary: Lieutenant James (United States of America)
(Plenary Session of the Conference of January 25, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Labor, Hôtel du Ministre, Dining Room
President: Mr. Samuel Gompers (United States of America)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. G. N. Barnes (British Empire);
The Rt. Hon. G. N. Barnes;

div.p: Secretary General: M. Arthur Fontaine (France)
Assistant Secretary General: Mr. H. B. Butler (British Empire)

row.cell: M. G. E. di Palma Castiglione.
Count G. de Hemricourt de Grunne.

div.p: Liaison with the Secretariat General of the Conference: M. J. Duboin.

p.hi: V. International Regime of Ports, Waterways, and Railways

div.p: (Plenary Session of the Conference of January 25, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Public Works, Room 39
President: M. Coromilas (Greece)
Liaison with the Secretariat General of the Conference: M. G. Lebre.
First Subcommission.—Questions Relating to Freedom of Transit
President: Hon. Henry White (United States of America)
Vice President: Sir Hubert Llewellyn Smith (British Empire)
Second Subcommission.—Regime of Ports, Waterways, and Railways
President: M. André Weiss (France)
(Sessions of the Supreme Council of the Allies of March 6 and April 16, 1919)
President: Hon. A. L. Sifton (British Empire)

row.cell: Rear Admiral Sir G. P. Hope.

div.p: (Sessions of the Supreme Council of the Allies of January 23 and March 1, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Finance (Hôtel du Ministre)
President: The Rt. Hon. E. S. Montagu (British Empire)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. E. S. Montagu;

div.p: Liaison with the Secretariat General of the Conference: M. J. Duboin.
(a) Urgent Problems Relating to the Preliminaries of Peace
President: M. S. Crespi (Italy)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. E. S. Montagu;
Lieut. Colonel the Hon. Sidney Peel;

div.p: (b) Special Subcommission on Monetary Questions
(In Liaison with the Commission on Reparation)
President: M. Kengo Mori (Japan)
Second Subcommission.—Monetary Questions
Third Subcommission.—Enemy Debts
(In Liaison with the Economic Commission)
The members of the Second and Third Subcommissions have not been designated.
Fourth Subcommission.—Interallied Problems and Project of Financial Section of the League of Nations
President: M. L. L. Klotz (France)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. E. S. Montagu;

div.p: Fifth Subcommission.—Payment of Austro-Hungarian Coupons
President: M. Édouard Benes (Czecho-Slovakia)
(Sessions of the Supreme Council of the Allies of January 27 and March 1, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Commerce (Hôtel du Ministre)
President: M. Clementel (France)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. Sir George Foster;

div.p: Secretary General: M. Serruys (France)
Liaison with the Secretariat General of the Conference: M. J. Duboin.
President: Sir Hubert Llewellyn Smith (British Empire)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. Sir George Foster.

div.p: Secretary: Mr. W. Carter (British Empire)
First Subcommission.—Customs Regulations, Duties, and Restrictions
President: Dr. Taussig (United States of America)
Secretary: Mr. W. Carter (British Empire)
Second Subcommission.—Treatment of Shipping
Secretary: Commander Thring, R. N.
Third Subcommission.—Unfair Methods of Competition
President: Mr. W. Temple Franks (British Empire)
Secretary: Mr. A. J. Martin (British Empire)
First Subcommission.—Industrial Property
President: Mr. W. Temple Franks (British Empire)
Secretary: Mr. A. J. Martin (British Empire)
Second Subcommission.—Pre-War Contracts
President: The Hon. C. J. Doherty (British Empire)
Secretary: Mr. W. W. Cumberland (United States of America)
Third Subcommission.—Liquidation of Enemy Property
President: M. Petit (France)
Secretary: Mr. W. W. Cumberland (United States of America)
President: M. A. Ricci-Busatti (Italy)
Secretary: Mr. Bertram F. Willcox (United States of America)
President: M. C. Dragoni (Italy)
Secretary: M. Teyssaire (France)
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of March 12, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Boulevard Saint-Germain, No. 282
President: General Duval (France)

row.cell: Major General the Rt. Hon. J. E. B. Seely;
Brigadier General P. R. C. Groves.
Colonel of the General Staff Thomas Dimitresco.

div.p: Secretary General: Lieut. Colonel Pujo (France)
Liaison with the Secretariat General of the Conference: Captain Roper.
1. Military Subcommission
Place of Meeting: Boulevard Saint-Germain, No. 199 bis
President: Brigadier General P. R. C. Groves (British Empire)
Vice President: Brigadier General Tanaka (Japan)

row.cell: Brigadier General P. R. C. Groves.

div.p: 2. Technical Subcommission
Place of Meeting: Boulevard Saint-Germain, No. 199 bis
President: Lieut. Colonel A. D. Butterfield (United States of America)
Vice President: Captain G. Finzi (Italy)
3. Judicial, Commercial, and Financial Subcommission
Place of Meeting: Rue de l’Université, No. 106
President: M. d’Aubigny (France)
Place of Meeting: Geographic Service of the Army, 140, Rue de Grenelle
President: General Bourgeois (France)
Secretary: Captain Le Maître (France)
Liaison with the Secretariat General of the Conference: M. E. Pépin.
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of April 19, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: M. Georges Cahen (France)

row.cell: Rear Admiral Sir George P. Hope;

div.p: Liaison with the Secretariat General of the Conference: M. L. de Montille.
III. Territorial Questions
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of February 27, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: M. André Tardieu (France)

row.cell: The Hon. H. Nicolson (for Bulgarian boundaries)
Colonel Pariani (for the boundary between Italy and Austria)
M. de Montille (Chargé of the Secretariat General)

div.p: Subcommission on Territorial Questions
(Appointed by the Central Territorial Committee)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
President: General Le Rond (France)
Secretary: M. de Montille (France)
1. Czecho-Slovak Affairs
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of February 5, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
President: M. Jules Cambon (France)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. Sir Joseph Cook;

div.p: Subcommission on Czecho-Slovak Affairs
(Session of the Commission on Czecho-Slovak Affairs of February 28, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: General Le Rond (France)

row.cell: Lieut. Colonel J. H. M. Cornwall.

div.p: Secretary: M. Lavondès (France)
2. Polish Affairs
(Sessions of the Supreme Council of the Allies of January 22 and 29, 1919)
President: M. Noulens (France)

row.cell: Brigadier General A. Carton de Wiart.

div.p: (Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of January 31, 1919)
President: M. Grenard (France)

row.cell: Lieut. Colonel R. P. Pakenham Walsh.

div.p: (Permanently at Paris)
(Sessions of the Supreme Council of the Allies of the Evening of February 12 and of February 26, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: M. Jules Cambon (France)
Subcommission for the Study of Eastern Frontiers
(Session of the Commission on Polish Affairs of March 20, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: General Le Rond (France)
Secretary: M. G. de Percin (France)
Subcommission for the Study of Western Frontiers
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of March 1, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: General Le Rond (France)
Secretary: M. G. de Percin (France)
Subcommission on Teschen and Orava
(Appointed by the Commission on Polish Affairs and the Commission on Czecho-Slovak Affairs)
(Sessions of the Supreme Council of the Allies of July 12 and August 7, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: General Le Rond (France)

p.hi: 4. Negotiation of an Armistice Between Poland and the Ukraine

div.p: (Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of April 16, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: . . . . . . .

row.cell: Commandant Escoffier (Chargé of the Secretariat General);

div.p: Liaison with the Secretariat General of the Conference: Commandant Escoffier.
Subcommission on Drafting
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: General Le Rond (France)
3. Eastern Frontiers of Germany
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of June 4, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: General Le Rond (France)
Secretary: M. G. de Percin (France)
4. Roumanian and Yugo-Slav Affairs
(With the exception of frontier questions common to the Yugo-Slav State and Italy)
(Sessions of the Supreme Council of the Allies of February 1 and 18, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: M. André Tardieu (France)
Vice President: M. G. de Martino (Italy)

row.cell: Mr. R. de Saint-Quentin (Chargé of the Secretariat General)

div.p: Subcommission on Roumanian and Yugo-Slav Affairs
(Decision of the Commission on Roumanian and Yugo-Slav Affairs of March 2, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: General Le Rond (France)

row.cell: Lieut. Colonel J. H. M. Cornwall.

div.p: Secretary: M. R. de Saint-Quentin (France)
5. Greek and Albanian Affairs
(Sessions of the Supreme Council of the Allies of February 4 and 24, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: M. Jules Cambon (France)
Vice President: The Rt. Hon. Sir Robert Borden (British Empire)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. Sir Robert Borden;

div.p: 6. Belgian and Danish Affairs
(Sessions of the Supreme Council of the Allies of February 12 and 21, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: M. André Tardieu (France)
Vice President: Sir Eyre Crowe (British Empire)
7. Baltic Affairs
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of April 16, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: Sir Esme Howard (British Empire)
(Assistants to the Commission for the Question of Access to the Baltic Sea and the North Sea)
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of August 2, 1919)
Secretary: M. de Montille (France)
IV. Special Questions in the Treaty With Germany
1. Drafting of the Military, Naval, and Aerial Clauses in the Treaty With Germany
President: General Degoutte (France)

row.cell: Rear Admiral Sir George P. Hope;
Brigadier General P. R. C. Groves;

div.p: Secretary: M. E. Carteron (France)
2. Submarine Cables
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of March 7, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: M. Henri Fromageot (France)
3. Morocco
(Council of Foreign Ministers of March 28, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: M. de Peretti de la Eocca (France)

row.cell: M. de Peretti de la Rocca.

div.p: 4. Left Bank of the Rhine
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of May 29, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs
President: M. Loucheur (France)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. the Lord Robert Cecil;
Commandant Escoffier (Chargé of the Secretariat General)

div.p: 5. Colonies
(Colonial Compensation Demanded by Italy from France and Great Britain)
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of May 7, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Colonies.
President: The Rt. Hon. Viscount Milner (British Empire)

row.cell: M. de Peretti de la Rocca.

div.p: Secretary General: M. Duchêne (France)
6. German Colonies
(Council of Foreign Ministers of April 23, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: M. de Peretti de la Rocca (France)

row.cell: M. de Peretti de la Rocca.

div.p: 7. Colonial Mandates
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies, June 27, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Colonial Office at London or Ministry of Colonies at Paris
President: The Rt. Hon. Viscount Milner (British Empire); or M. H. Simon (France).
Secretary: Mr. Philip Baker (British Empire)
8. Interallied Commission on the Rhine Territory
(Which Succeeds the “Interallied High Commission on the Rhine Territory”)
Place of Meeting: Paris and Coblenz
President: M. Tirard (France)
V. Special Questions in the Treaty With Austria
1. Morocco
See Special Questions in the Treaty with Germany.
2. New States
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies, May 1, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: M. Berthelot (France)
Liaison with the Secretariat General of the Conference: M. de Montille

(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of July 11, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: M. Laroche (France)
Secretary: M. J. Pieyre (France)

(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of June 6, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: M. S. Pichon (France)
Secretary: M. R. de Saint-Quentin (France)
5. Reparation Clauses in the Treaties With Austria, Hungary and Bulgaria
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of May 23, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Finance

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. the Lord Cunliffe;
The Rt. Hon. the Lord Sumner of Ibstone;
Lieut. General the Hon. J. C. H. Smuts;

div.p: Secretary: M. Foberti (Italy)
6. Tientsin

p.hi: Request for Cession to Italy of the Austrian Concession

div.p: (Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of July 12, 1919)
President: M. J. Gout (France)
Secretary: M. R. de Saint-Quentin (France)
VI. Examination of the Observations of the Delegations of Enemy Powers on Conditions of Peace
1. Committees for the Study of the Observations of the German Delegation on the Conditions of Peace
(Sessions of the Supreme Council of the Allies of April 30 and May 10, 1919)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. the Lord Robert Cecil.

row.cell: M. de Peretti de la Rocca.

Brigadier General P. R. C. Groves.

row.cell: Brigadier General P. R. C. Groves.

div.p: 3. Committees for the Coordination of Replies to the Counter Propositions

p.hi: 1. Committee for the Coordination of the Replies to the German Counter Propositions

div.p: (Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of June 12, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
President: M. André Tardieu (France)
Secretary: M. A. Portier (France)

p.hi: 2. Committee jor the Coordination oj the Replies to the Austrian Counter Propositions

div.p: (Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of July 29, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: M. Jules Cambon (France)
Secretary: M. A. Portier (France)
VII. Execution of the Treaty of Peace With Germany

1. Execution of the Clauses of the Treaty of Peace With GermanyThe other Commissions appointed by the Supreme Council of the Allies to assure the execution of the various clauses of the Treaty of Peace with Germany and the functioning of which is arranged only for a date after the putting into force of this Treaty will be made the subject matter of a separate part. [Footnote in the original.]
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of June 26, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: M. André Tardieu (France)
Secretary: M. A. Portier (France)
(a) Subcommission on the Organization of Commissions of Delimitation
President: General Le Rond (France)
Secretary: M. de Percin (France)
2. Rhine Territory
(Letter from the President of the Conference of Peace of June 7, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Industrial Reconstruction
President: M. Loucheur (France)
3. Destruction of the German Fleet at Scapa Flow
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of June 28, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
Liaison with the Secretariat General of the Conjerence: M. J. Duboin.
4. Repatriation of Prisoners of War
(Commission Appointed August 27, 1919)
President: M. Alphand (France)

row.cell: Lieut. General Sir H. E. Belfield.

div.p: Secretary: M. L. de Montille (France)
5. Repatriation of German, Austrian, and Hungarian Prisoners of War in Siberia
(Sessions of the Supreme Council of the Allies of September 27 and October 20, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: Colonel A. J. Logan (United States of America)

row.cell: Lieut. Colonel C. H. G. Black;

div.p: Secretary: M. P. Bressy (France)
VIII. Revision of International Acts
1. Revision of the General Acts of Berlin of 1885 and of Brussels of 1890
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of June 25, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Colonies
President: M. de Peretti de la Rocca (France)

row.cell: M. de Peretti de la Rocca;

div.p: Liaison with the Secretariat General of the Conference: M. Massigli.

Place of Meeting: Ministry of Colonies
President: M. de Peretti de la Rocca (France)

row.cell: M. de Peretti de la Rocca;

div.p: Liaison with the Secretariat General oj the Conference: M. Massigli.

(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of June 25, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Colonies
President: M. de Peretti de la Rocca (France)

row.cell: M. de Peretti de la Rocca.

div.p: Liaison with the Secretariat General of the Conference: M. Massigli.
IX. Questions of Different Kinds
1. Spitsbergen
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of July 7, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: M. Laroche (France)
Secretary: M. de Montille (France)
2. Revision of the Treaties of 1839
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of July 4, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
President: M. Laroche (France)

row.cell: Jonkheer R. de Marees van Swinderen;
M. R. de Saint-Quentin (Chargé of the Secretariat General).

div.p: Subcommission on Military and Naval Questions
President: Captain Le Vavasseur (France)
X. Economic Questions of Interest to the Allied and Enemy Countries
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of February 10, 1919
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Commerce (Hôtel du Ministre)
President: (The Different Members in Succession)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. Sir Joseph Maclay (Shipping);
The Rt. Hon. Sir Albert Stanley;
The Rt. Hon. the Lord Inverforth (Commerce and Blockade);
The Rt. Hon. Sir George Foster;
The Rt. Hon. W. M. Hughes.
The Rt. Hon. W. F. Massey.
The Rt. Hon. E. S. Montagu.

div.p: Place of Meeting: Ministry of Finance at Paris
President: Mr. Norman H. Davis (United States of America)
Secretary: Mr. H. A. Siepmann (British Empire)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Commerce at Paris
President: Mr. Bernard M. Baruch (United States of America)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. the Lord Robert Cecil;

div.p: Secretary: M. Simiand (France)
(Allied Maritime Transport Executive)
Secretary: Mr. Guthrie (British Empire)
President: Mr. Herbert Hoover (United States of America)
Place of Meeting: Alternately, Ministry of Public Works, Paris, Board of Trade, London
President: Brigadier General H. O. Mance (British Empire)
Secretary: Major N. H. Thornton (British Empire)
(Meeting at London: Trafalgar House, Waterloo Place)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Commerce, at Paris, or Board of Trade, at London
(Session of the Supreme Council of Allies of August 5, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Industrial Reconstruction
President: M. Loucheur (France)
XI. Committees on the Organization of Conferences, Commissions and Organizations Provided for in the Treaty of Peace
1. Committee on the Organization of the League of Nations
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of April 28, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Hotel de Crillon
President: M. S. Pichon (France)

row.cell: The Rt. Hon. the Lord Robert Cecil.

div.p: Liaison with the Secretariat General of the Conference: M. A. Portier.
2. Committee on the Organization of the International Labor Conference
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of April 11, 1919)
Place of Meeting: London
President: M. Arthur Fontaine (France)

row.cell: M. G. E. di Paima Castiglione.

div.p: Liaison with the Secretariat General of the Conference: Baron J. Pieyre.
3. Committee on the Organization of the Reparations Commission
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of July 2, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Ministry of Industrial Reconstruction, 136, Avenue des Champs-Élysées, Telephone Élysées 48–64
President: M. Loucheur (France)
Rue Pierre-Charron, 59, Telephone Élysées 24–64
XII. Commissions of Inquiry
1. Issues of Laibach
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of March 8, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Laibach
2. Fiume Incidents
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of July 7, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Fiume
President: Major General Sir H. E. Watts (British Empire)

row.cell: Major General Sir H. E. Watts.

div.p: 3. Issues of Smyrna
(Session of the Supreme Council of the Allies of July 21, 1919)
Place of Meeting: Constantinople
